title: crotalaria valida
intro: Crotalaria valida, also known as the white bush pea or white wedelia, is a plant species in the legume family. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Crotalaria valida include the treatment of various conditions such as fever, headache, and skin infections, as well as its potential use in wound healing and as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or decoction, which is believed to have therapeutic effects when consumed.
